patch.value: Declare trusted=true, sandbox_profile, and permissions on all 9 first-party package manifests

All 9 first-party package manifests (pm-beads, pm-calendar, pm-governance-audit, pm-guide-shell, pm-lifecycle-hooks, pm-linked-test-adapters, pm-search-advanced, pm-templates, pm-todos) are missing `trusted`, `sandbox_profile`, and `permissions` fields. These are the very features that extension governance policy evaluates. First-party packages serve as exemplars for third-party authors — if they don't declare these fields, authors copy the pattern and produce unsigned, unprofileable packages. The governance-audit manifest in particular should set sandbox_profile since it intentionally hooks onRead/onWrite. Evidence: all 9 manifests confirmed via audit 2026-06-07.

- All 9 first-party manifests declare `trusted`, `sandbox_profile`, and `permissions`
- governance-audit manifest declares a sandbox_profile consistent with its onRead/onWrite hooks
- Governance test enforces presence of these fields on first-party manifests

value.author: packages-agent
value.text: Starting: declaring trusted/sandbox_profile/permissions across all 9 first-party extension manifests. Reading extension-policy.ts for exact keys first.

value.author: packages-agent
value.text: Policy facts (extension-policy.ts + loader.ts): manifest fields trusted:boolean, sandbox_profile in none|restricted|strict, permissions keys fs_read/fs_write/network/env_read/env_write/process_spawn (loader rejects unknown non-boolean values). restricted disallows process_spawn+env_write; strict additionally network+fs_write; non-none profile without a permissions object = sandbox_permissions_missing. Chosen matrix: beads/todos/templates/governance-audit/search-advanced = restricted (mutate tracker or write files via SDK; search-advanced also network:true for embedding-backed reindex); calendar/guide-shell = strict (read-only views, fs_read+env_read only); lifecycle-hooks = strict (inert, all permissions false); linked-test-adapters = none + process_spawn:true (test-runs resume delegates to spawnBackgroundTestRunWorker, so restricted/strict would be violated). trusted:true everywhere.

value.author: packages-agent
value.text: DONE: all 9 first-party extension manifests now declare trusted:true + sandbox_profile + full 6-key permissions (fs_read/fs_write/network/env_read/env_write/process_spawn). Profiles: restricted=beads,todos,templates,governance-audit,search-advanced (tracker/file writes; search-advanced also network:true for embedding reindex); strict=calendar,guide-shell (read-only),lifecycle-hooks (inert, all false); none=linked-test-adapters (test-runs resume spawns background worker via SDK, so process_spawn:true is incompatible with restricted/strict). Governance test merged into tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts: asserts the 3 fields on every packages/*/extensions/*/manifest.json, exact 6 permission keys all-boolean, AND profile-consistency mirroring resolvePolicySandboxReason (restricted forbids process_spawn/env_write; strict additionally network/fs_write). package-manifest.spec.ts 16/16 green; real loader accepted the new manifest fields end-to-end (dist install-all integration test green).

value.author: codex-implementation-agent
value.text: Final verification evidence: pnpm build, pnpm typecheck, pnpm contracts:check, pnpm quality:static, pnpm quality:docs-links, pnpm security:scan, pnpm audit --audit-level low, node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (149 files, 2619 tests, 100% statements/branches/functions/lines), pnpm changelog:pm:check, pm health --check-only --summary, pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift, Sentry/telemetry gate (critical=0 high=0 telemetry ok), and isolated temp smoke for command-kit install/echo/list flag/package doctor all passed. Residual validation warning is the known pre-existing 116 older closed items missing resolution fields; history drift is clean.

patch.value: Added trusted, sandbox_profile, and permissions declarations to all first-party manifests.
Every first-party manifest declares trust and permission posture consistently.
All nine existing first-party manifests now declare trusted=true, sandbox_profile, and permissions; manifest tests passed.
First-party package manifest trust, sandbox, and permissions declarations completed.
